title: The Father of the Microchip
story: Beep, boop! Your computer's brain owes a lot to Geoffrey Dummer. Born in Hull, England on February 25, 1909, this brilliant electronics engineer would change the world. Dummer built the very first integrated circuit, also known as a microchip. These tiny marvels power our phones, laptops, and countless gadgets today. He also worked on radar systems, helping keep people safe during his long career. Dummer's inventions paved the way for our digital age.
activity_question: What did Geoffrey Dummer build that was a first of its kind?
follow_up_question: Can you name a device you use that has a microchip inside?

story: Buried secrets of an ancient civilization came to light in 1987. story: A storyteller was born! In 1842, Karl May entered the world in Ernstthal, Saxony. He grew up to become a famous German writer, creating thrilling adventures set in the American Wild West. His most beloved characters, Old Shatterhand and Winnetou, captured readers' imaginations on February 25 and for years to come.
activity_question: What type of stories did Karl May become famous for writing?
activity_choices: Thrilling adventures set in the American Wild West
Science fiction tales about space travel
activity_answer: Thrilling adventures set in the American Wild West
follow_up_question: Have you ever read a story set in the Wild West? What did you like about it?

title: Molecular Biology Pioneer Born
story: A scientific star was born on February 25, 1898. William Astbury entered the world in Longton, England. This future physicist and chemist would become a trailblazer in molecular biology. Astbury's work helped unlock the secrets of life's building blocks. His research laid the groundwork for understanding DNA structure.
activity_question: What field of science did William Astbury pioneer?
follow_up_question: Can you guess what 'molecular' means in molecular biology?
